channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: GP Discusses Pay, Health Advice, and the Realities of Modern Practice
item.description: Dr George Hawche, a GP based in west London, shares insights from his career in general practice, offering advice on health, lifestyle, and the NHS. He explains that salaried GPs earn between £80,000 ...

item.title: A Look at the Renewed Fears of Nuclear War in Today's World
item.description: The threat of nuclear war has been a distant concern for many years, but recent global tensions have brought it back into public conversation. Over 30 years ago, the prospect of a nuclear armageddon s...

item.title: 2 NPA Members Killed in Surigao Encounter After Collecting 'Revolutionary Tax'
item.description: Two members of the New People's Army (NPA), reportedly involved in collecting ‘revolutionary tax’ from local residents, were killed during a brief gunfight with government soldiers in Barangay Nurcia,...

item.title: PM Modi Addresses West Asia Crisis, Highlights India's Stand on Iran War and Hormuz Impact
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi recently addressed the Lok Sabha, discussing the ongoing conflict in West Asia that started on February 28 when the United States and Israel launched joint attacks on Iran...

item.title: Transgender activists protest in Hyderabad over amendment bill, seek policy changes
item.description: In Hyderabad, a significant protest was staged by trans rights activists opposing the proposed Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026. The demonstrators demanded the immediate...

item.title: Pak-China Relations Strengthened with Shared Development Goals, Says Xi
item.description: On the occasion of Pakistan Day, Chinese President Xi Jinping sent warm congratulations to President Asif Ali Zardari, emphasising China’s commitment to work alongside Pakistan to build a stronger com...

item.title: Concerns over police response in Canadian self-defence cases
item.description: Recent incidents in Ontario have reignited discussion about self-defence laws in Canada. In Vaughan, a homeowner shot and injured one of three armed and masked intruders who forced their way into his ...

item.title: Four Hatzola ambulances set ablaze in antisemitic arson attack in London
item.description: In the early hours of Monday morning, four ambulances belonging to Hatzola, a Jewish volunteer ambulance service based in north-west London, were deliberately set on fire in what authorities are treat...

item.title: How the US-Israel war against Iran is impacting the Philippines and Asia
item.description: The article by Glenda M. Gloria discusses how the escalating US-Israel war on Iran is creating ripple effects across the globe, particularly in the Philippines. The conflict, now in its fourth week, h...

item.title: Habermas and the crisis of democratic deliberation in the digital age
item.description: The article reflects on the relevance of Jürgen Habermas’s theory of deliberative democracy in contemporary societies dominated by digital media, polarization, and algorithmic attention economies. It ...

item.title: Four Jewish community ambulances set alight in Golders Green, London
item.description: Early on Monday morning, four ambulances operated by Hatzola, a Jewish volunteer ambulance service in Golders Green, north London, were deliberately set on fire. Police are treating the incident as an...

item.title: GrapheneOS Maintains Privacy-Focused OS Without Age Verification Requirements
item.description: GrapheneOS, an Android-based operating system designed with privacy in mind, has publicly stated that it will not implement age verification measures as required by new laws, such as Brazil's Digital ...

item.title: Kwankwaso Calls on NNPP Members to Stay Loyal and United Ahead of 2027
item.description: The 2023 presidential candidate for the New Nigeria Peoples Party (NNPP), Rabiu Musa Kwankwaso, don tell im followers and political allies make dem no lose focus for di common vision wey dey guide di ...

item.title: Kakamega MP Rewards Girl After She Outdances Him at School Event
item.description: At a school event in Kakamega, Malava MP David Ndakwa found himself outshone by a young girl during a lively dance competition. The event featured a Luhya song, 'Moyo Kwakhenya,' where both the MP and...

item.title: NLP Challenges Azimio Leadership Changes as Coalition Infighting Deepens
item.description: The National Liberal Party (NLP) has heightened its opposition to recent leadership changes within the Azimio la Umoja One Kenya Coalition, calling the move unconstitutional and politically motivated....

item.title: Court Rules Late Mosiuoa Lekota Lacked Mental Capacity, Questions Lover’s Financial Actions
item.description: The Gauteng High Court has delivered a scathing judgment concerning the financial dealings surrounding the estate of the late Cope leader, Mosiuoa Lekota. The court found that Lekota was not of sound ...

item.title: Sophie Kasaei speaks out as boyfriend Jordan Brook fights viral meningitis and encephalitis in hospital while she is pregnant
item.description: Television personality Sophie Kasaei, 31, has described her current situation as a 'living nightmare' after her boyfriend, TOWIE star Jordan Brook, was admitted to hospital with severe health complica...

item.title: Bagamoyo Students Face Long Walks, Financial Strain in Pursuit of Education
item.description: In Bagamoyo District, despite the Tanzanian government's plan to build a secondary school in every administrative ward, thousands of students still walk long distances to access education. Students in...

item.title: London: Jewish Ambulance Service Targeted in Arson Attack, Investigation Underway
item.description: A volunteer Jewish ambulance service in London has been targeted in a suspected arson attack, causing significant damage to four vehicles and prompting a police investigation. The incident occurred ea...

item.title: Poll Finds Ontarians Concerned About Ford Government’s Handling of Key Issues
item.description: As Ontario's Members of Provincial Parliament return following a 14-week winter recess, a recent Ipsos poll conducted exclusively for Global News' Focus Ontario suggests that a majority of Ontarians f...
